description: config.py loads LLM_API_KEY, LLM_MODEL, LLM_BASE_URL from .env via python-dotenv
verification: Create a .env file with test values: LLM_API_KEY=test-key, LLM_MODEL=test-model, LLM_BASE_URL=http://test-url

description: config.py provides defaults for LLM_TEMPERATURE, LLM_MAX_TOKENS, WORK_DIR, MAX_FIX_ATTEMPTS, DOCKER_BUILD_TIMEOUT, CONTAINER_STARTUP_TIMEOUT, LOG_LEVEL
config.py LLM_BASE_URL defaults to None when not set in .env
EnvVarSpec model validates correctly with all fields
verification: Create EnvVarSpec(name='API_KEY', description='API key', required=True, default_value=None, source='README', category='api_key')

description: RepoAnalysisResult model accepts all required fields and optional fields
verification: Create a RepoAnalysisResult with all required fields populated
Assert exposed_port defaults to None when not provided
Assert required_env_vars is a list of EnvVarSpec

description: DockerfileSpec model validates correctly
verification: Create DockerfileSpec with dockerfile_content, env_vars_to_inject, expected_transport, exposed_ports, entrypoint, notes

description: BuildResult model validates correctly
verification: Create BuildResult(success=False, image_tag='test:latest', build_log='ERROR', error_summary='missing package')

description: TestResult model validates correctly
verification: Create TestResult(container_started=True, startup_log='started', mcp_handshake_ok=True, error_summary=None, missing_secrets=[])

description: FinalOutput model validates correctly with all fields
verification: Create FinalOutput with all required fields
Assert instance.required_secrets is a list of EnvVarSpec

description: PipelineState enum has all required states
PipelineContext model has correct defaults and accepts all fields
MessageThread correctly initializes and manages messages
FunctionCallIntent stores tool call information
ModelClient initializes with config values and has correct interface
verification: Assert client uses config.LLM_API_KEY, config.LLM_MODEL, config.LLM_BASE_URL

description: ModelClient.chat_completion accepts messages list, tools list, and returns response
verification: Verify chat_completion method signature accepts messages: list[dict], tools: list[dict] | None, temperature: float | None
Verify method returns an OpenAI ChatCompletion-compatible response object

description: BaseAgent is abstract and requires subclasses to implement init_msg_thread and get_available_tools
verification: Assert TypeError is raised (cannot instantiate abstract class)
Create a minimal subclass implementing init_msg_thread and get_available_tools

description: BaseAgent manages message thread with add_system_message and add_user_message
verification: Create a concrete subclass of BaseAgent
Assert msg_thread contains both messages with correct roles

description: BaseAgent.should_stop returns True when round count exceeds max_round
verification: Create a concrete subclass with max_round=3

description: BaseAgent.execute_tool dispatches to registered tool functions
verification: Create a concrete subclass with a mock tool registered
Create a FunctionCallIntent for the mock tool
Assert the mock tool was called with correct arguments
Assert the result is returned as a string

description: BaseAgent.get_tool_definitions returns OpenAI-compatible tool schemas
verification: Create a concrete subclass with tools registered
Assert return is a list of dicts
Assert each dict has 'type': 'function' and 'function' key with 'name', 'description', 'parameters'

description: list_directory_tree returns ASCII tree of a directory
verification: Create a temp directory with subdirs and files
Assert result is a non-empty string
Assert result contains the subdirectory and file names

description: read_file returns content of a known file with line limit
verification: Create a temp file with 1000 lines of content
Assert result contains the file content

description: write_file creates or overwrites a file with given content
verification: Read the file back and assert content == 'hello world'
Read back and assert content == 'new content'

description: search_files finds files matching glob pattern
verification: Create a temp directory with files: a.py, b.py, c.txt
Assert result contains a.py and b.py
Assert result does not contain c.txt

description: search_in_files finds content matching regex across files
verification: Create temp files with known content including 'API_KEY' in one file
Assert result is a list of dicts with file, line_number, line keys
Assert the file containing 'API_KEY' is in the results

description: docker.from_env() successfully connects to local Docker Engine
build_image builds a Docker image from a Dockerfile and returns BuildResult
verification: Create a temp dir with a minimal Dockerfile (FROM alpine:latest)
Clean up: remove the built image

description: build_image returns BuildResult with success=False on invalid Dockerfile
verification: Create a temp dir with an invalid Dockerfile (FROM nonexistent_image_xyz:latest)
Assert returned BuildResult.error_summary is not None

description: run_container starts a container and returns container_id
verification: Build a minimal image (alpine with sleep command)
Assert returned container_id is a non-empty string
Clean up: stop and remove container and image

description: get_container_logs returns logs from a running container
verification: Start a container that produces known output (echo hello)
Assert result contains the expected output

description: stop_and_remove_container cleans up a container
verification: Assert container no longer exists (docker inspect fails)

description: remove_image removes a Docker image
send_mcp_initialize constructs correct JSON-RPC initialize message
verification: Assert the initialize request contains jsonrpc='2.0', id=1, method='initialize'
Assert params include protocolVersion, capabilities, clientInfo

description: RepoAnalysisAgent initializes with correct tools registered
RepoAnalysisAgent has system prompt covering structure analysis and env var scanning
verification: Assert SYSTEM_PROMPT is a non-empty string
Assert SYSTEM_PROMPT mentions project structure analysis
Assert SYSTEM_PROMPT mentions environment variable scanning
Assert SYSTEM_PROMPT mentions transport type detection (stdio/sse)

description: RepoAnalysisAgent.run() returns a valid RepoAnalysisResult
verification: Call run() with the python-sdk test repo path (C:\work_dir\testing_projects\python-sdk)
Assert result.language is a non-empty string
Assert result.package_manager is a non-empty string
Assert result.entry_point is a non-empty string

description: RepoAnalysisAgent correctly identifies Python project characteristics
verification: Run RepoAnalysisAgent on python-sdk test repo

description: RepoAnalysisAgent generates env_file_template content
verification: Run RepoAnalysisAgent on python-sdk test repo
Assert result.env_file_template is a non-empty string

description: DockerfileAgent initializes with correct tools registered
DockerfileAgent has system prompt with Dockerfile best practices
verification: Assert SYSTEM_PROMPT mentions Docker layer caching

description: DockerfileAgent.generate() produces a valid DockerfileSpec from RepoAnalysisResult
verification: Create a mock RepoAnalysisResult for a Python project
Assert dockerfile_content is a non-empty string containing 'FROM'

description: DockerfileAgent.fix() produces updated DockerfileSpec given error feedback
verification: Create a mock BuildResult(success=False, error_summary='missing package foo')
Assert returned dockerfile_content differs from the original

description: DockerfileAgent.generate() writes Dockerfile to disk
verification: Create a mock RepoAnalysisResult with a valid work_dir
Assert a Dockerfile file was written to the work_dir
Assert file content matches dockerfile_content in the returned DockerfileSpec

description: BuildTestAgent initializes with correct tools registered
BuildTestAgent.run() returns BuildResult and TestResult tuple
verification: Create a mock DockerfileSpec with a valid minimal Dockerfile
Assert return is a tuple of (BuildResult, TestResult)

description: BuildTestAgent returns BuildResult(success=False) and skips test on build failure
verification: Create a DockerfileSpec with an invalid Dockerfile

description: BuildTestAgent cleans up containers after test
verification: Run BuildTestAgent with a valid Dockerfile
After run completes, verify no leftover containers from the test exist

description: PipelineContext initializes with INIT state
Orchestrator transitions through pipeline states correctly on success path
verification: Mock RepoAnalysisAgent to return valid result with confidence > 0.3
Mock DockerfileAgent to return valid DockerfileSpec
Mock BuildTestAgent to return success BuildResult and TestResult with mcp_handshake_ok=True

description: Orchestrator aborts when RepoAnalysisAgent returns confidence < 0.3
verification: Mock RepoAnalysisAgent to return result with confidence=0.1

description: Orchestrator triggers fix loop on build failure
verification: Mock BuildTestAgent to fail first time, succeed second time
Mock DockerfileAgent.fix() to return updated DockerfileSpec

description: Orchestrator respects max_fix_attempts limit
verification: Assert notes mention max fix attempts reached

description: Orchestrator treats secrets-only failures as success with warnings
verification: Mock BuildTestAgent to return success build but TestResult with mcp_handshake_ok=False and missing_secrets=['API_KEY']
Assert notes contain warning about secrets

description: Orchestrator assembles correct FinalOutput with all fields
verification: Assert FinalOutput has non-empty startup_command containing 'docker run'

description: CLI parses all required arguments correctly
verification: from src.main import parse_args or build parser

description: CLI writes output files to output directory
verification: Run CLI with mocked orchestrator returning successful FinalOutput

description: Full pipeline happy path: python-sdk repo produces successful FinalOutput
verification: Assert FinalOutput.success == True or FinalOutput notes only mention secrets
Assert Dockerfile was written to output directory
Assert .env.example was written to output directory
Assert build-report.json was written to output directory

description: Full pipeline produces valid Dockerfile that builds successfully
verification: Run full pipeline on python-sdk repo
Assert Dockerfile contains ENTRYPOINT or CMD
Assert the Docker image was built (BuildResult.success == True)

description: Full pipeline fix loop recovers from initial build failure
verification: If the first build attempt fails, verify the fix loop activates
Assert DockerfileAgent.fix() is called with error details
Assert the pipeline retries build with corrected Dockerfile
Assert fix_attempts > 0 in the pipeline context
Assert pipeline eventually reaches DONE or FAILED state

description: Full pipeline generates correct .env.example with identified env vars
verification: Run full pipeline on python-sdk repo
Assert file contains variable names with placeholder values
Assert file has comment lines explaining variables

description: Full pipeline build-report.json contains complete execution trace
verification: Run full pipeline on python-sdk repo
Assert JSON is valid and parses to FinalOutput schema

description: Pipeline cleans up all Docker resources after completion
verification: After completion, list all containers with the test label/prefix
Assert no leftover containers from the pipeline run
Verify cleanup was performed in finally blocks
